title: Misc Arts
intro: Misc Arts questions gather disciplines that fall outside painting, sculpture, and the other named Fine Arts units — chiefly performance art, fashion, world music, and decorative craft. Performance and conceptual art form the largest cluster, led by Marina Abramovic's endurance pieces and the Fluxus collective (Yoko Ono, Joseph Beuys), alongside conceptual photographers and text artists like Cindy Sherman and Barbara Kruger who use the body or staged image to interrogate identity and consumerism.

Fashion supplies a second major cluster, spanning historic couturiers (Christian Dior's New Look, Elsa Schiaparelli's Surrealist collaborations) through contemporary designers (Alexander McQueen, Versace, Comme des Garcons) and the accessories and garments associated with them. World musical instruments and traditions — gamelan, sitar, didgeridoo — appear alongside general musical vocabulary like rhythm and drums, testing familiarity with non-Western musical cultures.

A smaller cluster covers decorative and functional crafts across civilizations: pottery, carpets, masks, silk, and terracotta recur as answerlines tied to specific cultural traditions rather than named individual artists. Public and installation art, represented by Ai Weiwei and Anish Kapoor, and countries, cities, and institutions — China, Japan, France, Tate Modern — round out the unit, each gathering clues about the traditions and artists associated with them.

sections.name: Performance and Conceptual Art
sections.blurb: Body, action, and idea-driven art that treats the artist or concept itself as medium.

entries.note: Serbian performance artist known for endurance pieces testing physical and psychological limits, including The Artist Is Present.
American conceptual artist known for bold text-over-image works critiquing consumerism and power, such as "Your body is a battleground."
International 1960s art collective blending performance, music, and visual art in playful, anti-commercial "events."
Japanese-American Fluxus-associated conceptual and performance artist; created Cut Piece and married John Lennon.
Pop artist who also produced experimental films and staged multimedia performances at his Factory studio.
Art form using the artist's body and live action as medium, exemplified by Marina Abramovic and the Fluxus artists.
Live entertainment tradition combining music, comedy, and drag, historically centered in Weimar-era Berlin clubs.
American conceptual photographer known for staged self-portraits critiquing media representations of women.
Performance tradition of exaggerated gender presentation, historically tied to cabaret and ballroom culture.
German Fluxus-associated artist known for performance pieces using felt, fat, and symbolic materials, including I Like America and America Likes Me.

sections.name: Fashion and Design
sections.blurb: Couture houses, designers, and garments treated as art objects.

entries.note: French fashion designer who founded his own house and popularized le smoking, the women's tuxedo suit.
British fashion designer known for dramatic, theatrical runway shows and provocative avant-garde tailoring.
Fashion accessory produced as a signature item by luxury houses like Hermes and Chanel.
Italian fashion house founded by Gianni Versace, known for bold prints and glamorous celebrity dressing.
French designer whose 1947 "New Look" redefined postwar women's fashion with a cinched waist and full skirt.
Japanese fashion house led by designer Rei Kawakubo, known for deconstructed, avant-garde silhouettes.
Italian-French designer known for Surrealist-influenced fashion collaborations with Salvador Dali, including the Lobster Dress.
Footwear elevated to art object by designers like Manolo Blahnik and Christian Louboutin.

sections.name: World Musical Instruments and Traditions
sections.blurb: Instruments and rhythmic traditions from non-Western musical cultures.

entries.note: Indonesian ensemble of tuned percussion instruments, including metallophones and gongs, central to Javanese and Balinese music.
Fundamental musical element organizing time, central to percussion-driven traditions like gamelan and drumming.
Long wind instrument of Aboriginal Australian origin, producing a continuous drone through circular breathing.
Percussion instruments central to rhythmic traditions across nearly every world culture.
Long-necked plucked string instrument central to Hindustani classical music, popularized in the West by Ravi Shankar.

sections.name: Ceramics, Textiles, and Decorative Crafts
sections.blurb: Functional and ceremonial objects made from clay, fiber, and glass across cultures.

entries.note: Furniture form frequently designed by artists and architects as functional sculpture.
Ceramic vessels shaped from clay, one of humanity's oldest decorative and functional art forms.
Woven textile floor coverings, especially prized in Persian and Islamic decorative art traditions.
Decorative and functional material shaped through blowing and casting, prominent in studio art glass traditions like Chihuly's.
Ceremonial and decorative objects worn across cultures, from African ritual masks to Venetian carnival masks.
Luxury textile fiber historically traded along the Silk Road and central to Chinese decorative craft.
Fired clay material used for sculpture and pottery, notably China's life-sized Terracotta Army.

sections.name: Public and Installation Art
sections.blurb: Large-scale sculpture and unsanctioned art made for public and civic space.

entries.note: British-Indian sculptor known for large-scale reflective public works like Cloud Gate ("The Bean") in Chicago.
Chinese conceptual artist and activist known for large-scale installations like Sunflower Seeds and outspoken government criticism.
Unsanctioned public wall art tradition that evolved into recognized street art through figures like Banksy and Jean-Michel Basquiat.

sections.name: Countries, Cities, and Institutions
sections.blurb: Nations, cities, and museums that organize clues across art forms and traditions.

entries.note: Home to traditional arts like ikebana and the postwar Gutai performance art group.
Home to the terracotta warrior sculptures and contemporary conceptual artist Ai Weiwei.
Hub of performance and conceptual art movements, home to Andy Warhol's Factory and countless galleries.
Home to Fluxus artist Joseph Beuys and a major postwar contemporary art scene.
Home to fashion houses Dior and Saint Laurent, centered in Paris.
Home to fashion houses Versace and Prada and a long design and craft tradition.
Language and script frequently incorporated into calligraphy and decorative Islamic art.
Home to tango culture and a distinct Latin American contemporary art scene.
Home to a distinct Afro-Caribbean art and music tradition shaped by its revolutionary history.
Home to hula dance and a distinct Polynesian craft and decorative art tradition.
Home to the sitar tradition and a long history of textile and carpet-weaving crafts.
Home to the terracotta warrior sculptures and contemporary conceptual artist Ai Weiwei.
London museum of international modern and contemporary art housed in a former power station.

appendix.answer: Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts
